a Bimboleum Emitter

A custom designed Bimboleum Emitter, like the kind Doctor Badger uses.\n\nMay cause fantasies, enflamed genitalia, inflated genitalia, orgasm, mild euphoria, severe euphoria, temporary loss of intelligence, permanent loss of intelligence, spontaneous silicone synthesis, drooling, undue vaginal secretions, sudden onset of fetishes, attention deficit disorder, high blood pressure, or cardiac arrest. Effects are generally temporary unless exposure is long term.
FGC RF
Fogcaster rifle
a Fogcaster assault rifle

The APR-F rifle, commonly known as “Fogcaster”, is a heavily modified, support-oriented version of the Apollo Plasma Rifle. Lighter and easier to handle, it can also fire standard UGC grenade rounds loaded with lust-inducing toxins thanks to its launcher add-on.\n\nUnlocks the Lust Grenade skill
plasma orb
Tachyon Beam
tachyon beam laser
a tachyon beam laser gun

An ultra-heavy beam laser designed for medium to long range engagements. Due to the power requirements of the laser emitters used by the weapon, it’s commonly found mounted to various forms of mechanized exoskeletal combat suits-- if not directly attached to the hull of a starship.\n\nBy introducing modulated tachyon pulses into the focusing chamber, the laser can impart some finite quantity of mass to photons directed towards the target aperture.
beam laser
broken Brainmelt Lamp
a broken Brainmelt Lamp
The deceptively innocuous pink-bulbed Brainmelt Lamp seems to have been damaged in the fight. You might be able to sell it for scrap.
BFD 9001
BFD 9001
a really big drill

By far the largest weapon available to UGC troops, the Brighton-Farnsworth Decimator 9001 spear-drill hybrid is at home dealing with low mobility, highly-armored targets. Powered up by savicite, its enormous next-generation alloy drill spins so fast and generates enough heat that it can tear a hole in a ship’s hull in seconds. Unwieldy to the point that’s pretty much unusable by a normal soldier not wearing power armor, the rear of its long handle is fitted with a miniaturized rocket for additional thrusting power.
steel cutlass
a steel cutlass

A fairly standard steel cutlass for the renaissance reenactor in all of us. This hefty sword is and has been a standard for close-quarters boarding actions since the age of sail and has analogs in most interstellar cultures. A favorite of pirates, raiders and navy officers the galaxy over.
dancing flashblade
a flashblade
Marked as ‘CV-x808: Dancing Flashblade’ along the back of the hilt, this unique prototype puts a spin on popular hardlight weapons. The 2' long blade is perfectly straight when triggered with
a glowing white shimmer covering its near-transparent edge. The ornate black-and-gold hilt appears to be custom fitted for someone else but still slides very comfortably into your hands all the same.
When wielding it, the weapon is ultra-light and easy to use but the low-substance blade is brittle and poor for parrying. It appears to be made of some nano-thin hardlight configuration and
when contact with an object is made, the blade discharges in brilliant white light only to shatter and reform in a split second.
It seems ripe for modification and as [pc.aStartingCareer], you’ve been able to
add some shielding to the hand-grip and mace tips to the end for knocking targets down.
It seems ripe for modification and as [pc.aStartingCareer], you’ve been able to
strip down the model for a lighter, much more nimble build.
It seems ripe for modification and as [pc.aStartingCareer], you’ve been able to
overcharge the current for more power.
PIP Blade
plasma blade
a Pyrite Industries plasma blade

Blades of pure plasma are nothing to mess with - even if they come with a half-empty power cell and a hilt scarred by the endless strikes of korgonne spears. This one still works, surprisingly, and the denizens of Korg'ii Hold saw fit to gift it to you for saving them.
burning slash
Widow’s Kiss
the Widow’s Kiss

Said to have been wielded by Shar Lot Ebb, the first and only bothrioc ruler of ancient Karachnarum, the blade of this viciously sharp, obsidian rapier appears to be made from bothrioc chitin. It has a small tuft of bluish fiber hanging from the pommel, and an organic jag near the hilt forms a nest-like guard around the handle. Using it gives you a strangely unstoppable, ravishing aura, your every move seemingly brilliantly, beautifully calculated. Lethally sexy.
a vial of lustoxin
A gold myr chemical weapon that explodes into a cloud of lusty toxins when shattered.
Using a chemical weapon without a target to throw it at would be pretty dumb now, wouldn’t it?
{var0}+{var1}+" considers it unwise to use a chemical weapon outside of combat.

original: scout shield
a compact shield generator that offers elemental protection

Designed with short individuals and explorers in mind, this compact and lightweight shield generator prioritizes agility and mobility over raw shield strength. It provides good protection from extreme temperatures, a must for venturing outside of Dhaal's inhabited areas.
Doctor's Bag
doctor’s bag
a bag filled with medical supplies

This briefcase-sized black leather bag is magnetically locked by two clasps at the top. Chief among its contents are a variety of fast-acting supplements that increase the user’s metabolism and their natural regeneration. Loaded next to those are bandages, rolls of gauze, medipens packed with painkilling injections, and a can of silvery medigel. This is a common assortment to be found on anyone training to be a nurse, or in someone’s medicine cabinet if they’re expecting more than the common cold.
This item should not be used on NPCs.
It’d be a bit silly to use medicine when you’re perfectly fine.
Back
You just about collapse under the weight of your sustained injuries,
carefully peeling off your [pc.armor], and inspect
inspecting
yourself for wounds and bruises after setting out the bag’s contents.

Wasting no time, you inject yourself with two painkillers and gulp down the provided pills, then turn your attention to two sucking wounds on your chest and abdomen. Thanks to the painkillers, you barely wince when you spray half the can of medigel on both wounds and soak up the blood with the supplied gauze. After discarding three squares of the fabric, you get them cleaned up enough to bandage.

You turn your attention to the less painful cuts and scrapes, then spritz what’s left of the regelatin on them before using the last of the gauze to wipe it away. With your [pc.skinFurScalesNoun] healing and your body’s regeneration beating double time, you regard your bruises with some annoyance.
You’re going to have to rest for your body to work its magic. The question then becomes: for how long can you wait?

You sit your weary self down and pop open the doctor’s bag you’ve been carrying around for just this occasion. You empty the contents in front of you and organize them into the order you will use them. First, a small bottle containing six colorful pills, some marked and some not. You take them all at once, and with a swig of water from your canteen they go down easily.
You then
remove your [pc.armor] next and

inspect yourself for any injuries. Fortunately you’re not in terribly bad shape. You’ve only got a few injuries worth griping over. The rest are superficial. You take one of the medipens and press it to your neck, not even feeling its payload penetrate your [pc.skinFurScalesNoun]. Discarding the tool, you reach for the gauze and regelatin, spraying your deeper wounds before wiping the blood away. You then use up the last of the gel spray on your smaller cuts, making very methodical applications.
Within seconds, your wounds begin to close and you feel a deep urge to rest. While you’re feeling better already, the effects won’t truly manifest unless you give it time to work.
1 Hours
2 Hours
3 Hours
4 Hours
Never Mind
This place is too dangerous for you to kick back and rest, let alone wait for some fancy meds to kick in.
Fly away, nerd!
field surgeon’s kit
a kit of medical spray

This handy kit comes pre-packaged with three cans of regelatin spray. Once applied to a wound, the gel is absorbed straight away and reconstitutes the damaged tissue by melding into the patient’s body. Flesh knitting rapidly is not a painless process without unwanted secondary effects, which is why it’s best used on light injuries and not gaping wounds.
Using supplements to heal yourself will only work once per fight. No body can handle the metabolic strain of downing multiple regenerative items in such quick succession.

I. Stim
illegal stimulant medipen
a medipen full of illegal stimulants

This self-contained injection system is loaded with potent drugs known to cause a rush of energy. While drugs like this are common with mercenaries, this one appears to have been crafted bespoke, for some specific purpose.\n\nKnown to cause moderate amounts of taint. Check your Codex for details.
knotted terran dildo
a knotted terran dildo

This no-name terran dildo bears no marks of manufacturer nor identification of any kind. The wobbly 7-inch toy’s only distinguishing feature is the presence of a nearly double-wide knot at the base, an obvious concession to appeal to ausars and terrans alike.\n\nAnd it’s neon green.
neon green
queenly necklace
a queenly necklace

This enormous necklace is clearly made for a giant of a woman, and weighs enough to nearly topple a normal person over if they chose to wear it. It won’t do you much good, but it’s definitely worth a pretty penny.
You feel a faint tickle at the back of your throat. Did the previous combat encounter get you sick? It might be nothing, but you could always get it checked out.
Sneezing Tits
You have been infected!\n\nCaution: Sporadic sneezing may cause spontaneous breast growth.
Your Codex beeps, alerting you that you’ve been infected with The Sneezing Tits! While your microsurgeons will be working full time to prevent you from spreading the virus around, sneezing
extra
breast tissue... Unless you get it treated, of course.
All of a sudden, your nose tickles, and you sneeze. There’s a strange billowing sensation from your chest. You catch your breath and look down at yourself.
Your nose feels itchy. There’s a little bit of a tingling sensation, and you almost feel like you have to sneeze but not quite. You sniff a few times, but the feeling doesn’t go away.

You lean back and make an “Ah–ah–aaah” noise, then let out a sneeze, not too loud but enough to draw notice. You take a deep breath once you’re sure you’re not going to sneeze again, and realize that your chest feels weightier than before.

Your nose is really itching now. You rub at it, and sniff over and over, even cough a few times trying to shake off the feeling. You can tell you need to sneeze, and take a few deep breaths trying to make it happen, to no avail.

The tingling in your nose finally becomes too much, and you sneeze loud enough to startle anyone nearby. You wipe at your eyes, breathing deep. Each breath comes with the realization of a considerably increased weight from your chest.

Your eyes are watering, and your nose feels like it’s rebelling against the rest of your body. What is going on with your sinuses? You need to sneeze, but no matter what you do, it’s just not happening.

The sneeze hits you all at once and harder than you’d thought possible. Your eyes squeeze shut as you bend yourself nearly in half at the sudden and violent exhalation, the sound so loud it echoes all around you. As you take a deep breath and attempt to recover, you feel a new and significant heaviness from your chest.

You’re not sure how it happened, but that sneeze made you grow breasts. Where you once had a flat chest, you now have a pair of [pc.breasts], slightly tender from the growth. It doesn’t seem like much of a difference, but you are now "+{var2}.indefiniteArticle({var1}.breastCup({var0}))+".
